frameworks.name: compositional Bayesian regression
frameworks.one_line: intuitive functions modeled as compositions of simpler building-block functions

frameworks.name: grammar over Gaussian process kernels
frameworks.one_line: kernel grammar formalizes how simple kernels combine into complex functions

frameworks.name: non-compositional function learning
frameworks.one_line: monolithic function priors lacking decomposition into reusable parts

frameworks.name: broad principles of human cognition
frameworks.one_line: compositionality as a general organizing principle across cognitive domains

core_question: Do people learn complex functions by composing simpler building blocks rather than as monolithic structures?
key_assumptions: complex function structure can be decomposed into simpler parts
Bayesian regression with kernel grammar captures human function priors
predictability judgments reflect underlying generative priors
manipulations: elicited samples from human prior over functions
predictability ratings for matched compositional vs non-compositional functions
key_findings: participants prefer compositional over non-compositional extrapolations
samples from human function priors are best fit by a compositional model
compositional functions are perceived as more predictable than non-compositional counterparts
interpretations: intuitive functions have a compositional structure
compositionality of function learning aligns with broad principles of human cognition
